name: wmfam_cumulative_state_tracking_probe
citation: Anonymous Authors. WMF-AM: Probing LLM Working Memory via Depth-Parameterized Cumulative State Tracking. NeurIPS 2026 Evaluations and Datasets Track (under review).
description: WMF-AM is a workload-parameterized no-scratchpad probe for cumulative state tracking in LLMs, inspired by working memory paradigms in cognitive science. The depth parameter K precisely controls per-query cognitive workload. A matched non-arithmetic cumulative logical probe (permissions, schedule, inventory tracking) validates cross-domain generalization. The release includes four construct-isolation ablations, extended K-sweep (K=3 to 100), load-shift intervention, and a 10-task deterministic agent battery. Results span 28 models (21 open-weight, 0.5B-70B; 7 closed API/LRM) from 12 families.

distribution.name: master_28models.csv
distribution.description: Per-model results for all 28 models: WMF-AM scores (K=3/5/7 mean and per-K extended sweep K=3..100), agent battery scores, sigmoid fit parameters (K_crit, alpha, R2), load-shift intervention results, MMLU and GSM8K baseline scores.

distribution.name: wmf_am_item_level_consolidated.csv
distribution.description: Item-level binary accuracy data for 1500 WMF-AM trials across 20 open-weight models. Columns: model, K, seed, surface_form, accurate (0/1), family, log_params.

distribution.name: probe_templates
distribution.description: WMF-AM probe generation code including all surface forms (points scoring, warehouse inventory, bank accounts), ablation variants (K=1, non-arithmetic, yoked cancellation), and extended K-sweep scripts.

distribution.name: cumulative_logical_probe
distribution.description: Non-arithmetic cumulative state tracking probe across 3 domains (permissions: binary flag set tracking; schedule: meeting count tracking; inventory: item set membership tracking). K-parameterized (K=3,5,7), matched structure to arithmetic WMF-AM. Results for all 28 models.

distribution.name: agent_battery
distribution.description: 10-task deterministic ReAct agent battery with deterministic rule-based scoring. Tasks span multi-step calculation, entity tracking, file search, fact verification, source attribution, conversation recall, shopping, scheduling, and data pipeline.

field.name: type
field.description: Model type: standard, LRM, or LRM-distill

field.name: K_crit
field.description: Critical depth from sigmoid fit (50% accuracy breakpoint)

rai:dataCollection: All data collected via deterministic API calls to language models (Ollama for open-weight, OpenRouter for closed API). No human subjects involved. Probe generation uses fixed random seeds for reproducibility.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: None. The dataset contains only model evaluation scores, not human data.
rai:dataSocialImpact: The benchmark evaluates LLM process-level capabilities for agent reliability assessment. No direct social harm anticipated.
rai:dataLimitations: Results are specific to the 28 models evaluated. Probe is limited to cumulative arithmetic state tracking; other process-level capabilities are not measured. Agent battery uses a single seed.
rai:dataBiases: The dataset evaluates 28 LLMs from 12 model families, with overrepresentation of certain families (6 Qwen, 4 DeepSeek, 4 Llama variants). Open-weight models are biased toward Ollama-hosted quantized versions, which may not reflect official checkpoint performance. API models (7/28) introduce uncontrolled confounds (unknown quantization, decoding strategy, internal chain-of-thought). Geographic and demographic bias is not applicable as the dataset contains no human-generated content — all data are deterministic model evaluation outputs on synthetic probes.
rai:dataUseCases: This dataset measures LLM cumulative state tracking fidelity under controlled depth-parameterized workload, operationalizing working memory capacity from cognitive science. Validated use cases: (1) cross-model comparison of cumulative tracking ability at varying depths K=3–100, (2) predicting downstream multi-step agent performance (τ=0.595, p<0.001, N=28), (3) construct-isolation analysis via ablation controls. Not validated for: clinical or safety-critical deployment decisions, fine-tuning, or evaluation of non-English language capabilities.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.prov:label: Deterministic LLM Evaluation
prov:wasGeneratedBy.sc:description: All evaluation data were collected via deterministic API calls (temperature=0, greedy decoding) to 28 language models: 21 open-weight models served locally via Ollama, 7 closed-source models via OpenRouter API. Probe instances were generated programmatically using fixed random seeds (seeds 42, 123, 456, 789) for reproducibility. Each model was administered six evaluation phases: WMF-AM core probe (K=3/5/7, 4 seeds × 15 items/depth), yoked cancellation control, template harmonization, 10-task deterministic agent battery, extended K-sweep (K=3–100), and non-arithmetic logical probe (3 domains × K=3/5/7). No manual annotation, crowdsourcing, or post-hoc filtering was applied. Raw JSON outputs are preserved in the repository.
